spots.name: Grayton Beach Surf
spots.notes: Walk-in surf access. Best on a rising tide with light onshore wind. Sand fleas and pompano jigs work the troughs.

spots.name: Western Lake (30A)
spots.notes: Coastal dune lake; brackish when the outflow opens. Mix of largemouth and inshore species — switch tackle based on the outflow state.

spots.name: Eastern Lake
spots.notes: Quieter dune lake. Topwater at first light along cypress edges.

spots.name: Phillips Inlet
spots.notes: Outflow zone — fish moving water on the bigger tide swings.

spots.name: Topsail Hill Preserve Beach
spots.notes: Longer walk to less-pressured surf. Park at the day-use entrance.

spots.name: Choctawhatchee Bay (North Shore)
spots.notes: Grass flats. Popping cork + soft plastic at dawn; switch to topwater when fish are crashing bait.

spots.name: Destin East Pass
spots.notes: Jetty rocks hold sheepshead year-round; live shrimp on a tight line.

spots.name: Fairhope Municipal Pier
spots.notes: Free public access on Mobile Bay. Best around tide change and the jubilee months.

spots.name: Dauphin Island (Pelican Point)
spots.notes: Sandy point. Pompano in spring; trout and reds the rest of the year.

spots.name: Reelfoot Lake
spots.notes: Legendary West TN crappie water. Cypress stumps and stake beds — go slow with light line.

spots.name: Norris Lake (Big Ridge)
spots.notes: Deep, clear reservoir. Fish points and bluffs in summer; spinnerbaits in the creeks in spring.

spots.name: Vidalia Bay (St. Joseph Bay)
spots.notes: Seagrass flats — wade or pole; sight-fish reds on the tail in 1–2 ft.
